title: First conditional
explanation:
First conditional

If + present simple, will + verb

We use the first conditional for real possibilities in the future.

  • If it rains, I will stay at home.
  • If you study, you will pass the exam.

You can also use unless (= if not): Unless you hurry, you will miss the bus.

questions.text: If it [*rains|will rain|rain], we will cancel the picnic.
If you [*don't hurry|won't hurry|not hurry], you will be late.
She [*will call|'ll calling|calls] you if she has time.
If I [*see|will see|seeing] Tom, I'll tell him.
Unless you [*ask|will ask|asked], they won't help.
If we [*leave|will leave|leaving] now, we will catch the train.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: If you heat ice, it ____. | *melts | will melt | melted
questions.feedback: Zero/first: present in both for facts.

questions.text: If she ____ hard, she will pass. | *works | will work | worked
I will be angry if you ____ that again. | *do | will do | did
If they don't arrive soon, we ____ without them. | *will start | start | started
If you [*mix|will mix|mixed] red and blue, you get purple.
I'll lend you money if you [*pay|will pay|paid] me back.
If he [*doesn't come|won't come|not come], call me.
We [*will have|'ll having|have] fun if the weather is good.
If I [*find|will find|found] your keys, I'll text you.
